response.justification: Candidate has strong geospatial domain expertise (GISCollective founder, Green Map, FOSS4G talk) and prior low-latency distributed systems experience (Sociomantic/Dunnhumby with DLang), which are transferable to a routing tile pipeline. However, the core requirement—modern C++ in commercial/OSS environments or equivalent Rust—is largely absent: only ~6 months of C/C++ at National Instruments (2014–2015) and a bachelor thesis, with no Rust and recent work centered on TypeScript/Node.js. Worth a screening call to assess depth of systems work and willingness to ramp on C++, but not a strong direct match.
strengths: Genuine geospatial/GIS background through GISCollective and Green Map
Low-latency, high-throughput systems experience at Sociomantic with DLang
Strong Linux, testing discipline (TDD), and pipeline/CI maturity
concerns: Very limited and dated modern C++ experience; no Rust
Recent five+ years focused on TypeScript/Node.js web stack, not systems programming
No demonstrated work on navigation/routing engines or tiling pipelines specifically
